name: Drunken Crawfish Pie
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Green onion tops, sliced thin
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt butter over low heat in a large skillet.
2. Gradually add flour and cook slowly, stirring constantly until a very light roux forms.
3. Add the chopped onion, garlic, green pepper, celery, parsley, and green onion tops. Continue cooking, stirring occasionally, until all vegetables are soft.
4. Stir in salt, black pepper, heavy cream, and brandy. Mix gently but thoroughly and cook for 3 additional minutes over low heat.
5. Add crawfish tails and cook for an additional 10 minutes or until the crawfish is tender. Remove from heat.
6. Allow filling to cool completely.
7. Spoon cooled filling into an unbaked pie crust shell. Place a top crust over the filled pie. Moisten and seal the edges. Flute as desired.
8. Cut 6 slits in the top crust. Bake at 350°F for 25-30 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown. Cool and serve.
